june.title: Three-Day June Itinerary
june.subtitle: Long days, outdoor time, historical depth

schedule.activity: Breakfast in Mitte. Walk to Alexanderplatz before 11:30.
Free walking tour (Tue to Sat). Two hours. Alexanderplatz to Hackescher Markt via Rotes Rathaus, TV Tower, Marx-Engels-Forum, Museum Island.
Lunch in Hackesche Höfe. Loop back to Museum Island for Neues Museum or Alte Nationalgalerie.
Dinner along the Spree. Sunset from a bridge. Light until 21:30+.
Brandenburg Gate, Reichstag dome (book ahead), slow walk through Tiergarten.
Coffee or beer at Café am Neuen See.
S-Bahn to Wannsee or Schlachtensee. Swim, lakeside walk, optional ferry.
Dinner in Charlottenburg or Mitte.

days.theme: The Wall and the alternative city

schedule.activity: Bernauer Straße Wall Memorial. Walk the preserved death strip.
Mauerpark (Sundays: flea market and karaoke).
East Side Gallery, then Tempelhofer Feld. Cycling on the runways at golden hour.
Outdoor cinema (Freiluftkino) or Friedrichshain beer garden.

july.title: Three-Day July Itinerary
july.subtitle: Early starts, shaded breaks, long summer evenings

days.theme: Historic core before the heat builds

schedule.activity: Start at Alexanderplatz while the city is still cooler. Coffee, water bottle, comfortable shoes.
Free walking tour (Tue to Sat). Two hours from Alexanderplatz to Hackescher Markt through the historic center.
Lunch near Hackescher Markt, then an indoor stop on Museum Island or a shaded break by the Spree.
Walk Unter den Linden toward Brandenburg Gate after the heat drops. Sunset stays late.

days.theme: Water, parks, and a cooler rhythm

schedule.activity: Reichstag dome if booked ahead, then Tiergarten before midday heat.
Long shaded lunch or cafe break. Do not force exposed sightseeing at 14:00.
S-Bahn to Schlachtensee, Wannsee, or another lake if the forecast is hot.
Beer garden, Spree walk, open-air cinema, or Classic Open Air if your dates match.

days.theme: The Wall, neighborhoods, and event-aware planning

schedule.activity: Berlin Wall Memorial at Bernauer Strasse before crowds and heat peak.
Prenzlauer Berg or Mitte lunch, then an indoor museum or hotel rest if it is hot.
East Side Gallery or a neighborhood walk, adjusted for CSD or major event closures.
Keep plans flexible. July evenings are better when you leave room for weather and events.

august.title: Three-Day August Itinerary
august.subtitle: Warm days, lake time, and event-aware evenings

days.theme: Historic core before the city heats up

schedule.activity: Start early at Alexanderplatz. Bring water and use the cooler part of the day for the historic center.
Free walking tour (Tue to Sat). Two hours from Alexanderplatz to Hackescher Markt through the city's oldest layers.
Lunch near Hackescher Markt, then choose an indoor stop on Museum Island or a shaded riverside break.
Spree walk, beer garden, open-air cinema, or a slow sunset loop through Mitte.

days.theme: Lake day without pretending Berlin is the Mediterranean

schedule.activity: Reichstag dome if booked ahead, then Tiergarten or a shaded neighborhood walk.
Travel to Schlachtensee, Wannsee, Plötzensee, or another lake before the hottest part of the afternoon.
Swim or picnic, but check water quality and expect crowds on hot weekends.
Return for dinner in Kreuzberg, Prenzlauer Berg, Friedrichshain, or Charlottenburg.

days.theme: The Wall, culture, and late-August events

schedule.activity: Berlin Wall Memorial at Bernauer Strasse before crowds and heat build.
Lunch and a cool-down break. August thunderstorms are a feature, not a failure.
East Side Gallery, Futurium, or a museum depending on weather.
If your dates match, plan around Tanz im August, Long Night of Museums, or the August 12 astronomy/eclipse evening.

september.title: Three-Day September Itinerary
september.subtitle: Walking weather, art season, and event-aware planning

days.theme: Historic core in the best walking weather

schedule.activity: Start at Alexanderplatz while the light is good and the temperature is comfortable.
Free walking tour (Tue to Sat). Two hours from Alexanderplatz to Hackescher Markt through the city's oldest layers.
Museum Island, Humboldt Forum, or a cafe break if September rain appears.
Spree walk, Gendarmenmarkt, or dinner before the evening cools down.
Prenzlauer Berg, Kreuzberg, or Charlottenburg, depending on where you are staying.
Lunch in a neighborhood rather than rushing back to the main tourist axis.
If your dates match Berlin Art Week, choose one gallery cluster or exhibition route.
Use the earlier sunset deliberately: viewpoint, riverside walk, gallery opening, or performance.

days.theme: The Wall, museums, and September event logistics

schedule.activity: Berlin Wall Memorial at Bernauer Strasse or East Side Gallery while the day is still bright.
Museum, lunch, or Open Monument Day stop if September 13 matches your dates.
Tiergarten, Reichstag Dome if booked, or a practical rest before the evening.
On Marathon weekend, avoid tight cross-city plans and let the city atmosphere be part of the day.
